content.full_text:
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY OF LONG BEACH

June 4, 1991

California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Request by Councilman Drummond for Written Advice Regarding Possible Disqualification

Dear Commission Staff:

Long Beach City Councilmember Douglas S. Drummond, who represents the Third Council District, has asked our office to request your written advice regarding his possible disqualification from participation in consideration of the revision of the Transportation (Circulation) Element of the City's General Plan which would, among many other things, reenact street improvement policies including a possible intersection improvement project in the vicinity of certain real property in which the Councilmember currently has a financial interest.

Mr. Drummond owns a two bedroom, two bath stock cooperative apartment at 21 Alamitos Avenue in Long Beach which is located within three hundred feet of the intersection of Alamitos Avenue and Ocean Boulevard. Councilman Drummond does not reside in this apartment but rents it to a tenant. It is not located within the boundaries of his councilmanic district.

In the near future, the City Council will be considering a comprehensive revision of the Transportation (Circulation) Element of its General Plan. The proposed revised element applies to traffic circulation within the entire City which consists of approximately 430,000 citizens and is comprised of about 50 square miles with over 830 miles of streets and approximately 9,000 intersections. In the revision of the element, the particular intersection of Ocean Boulevard and Alamitos Avenue is identified as the location for a grade separation project where those streets will be reconstructed to intersect on two different levels. Said grade separation project would cost a substantial amount of public funds to complete and, arguably, will have some financial effect on the value of properties within

California Fair Political Practices Commission
June 4, 1991
Page 2

close proximity to the intersection. Furthermore, there is a good likelihood that Councilman Drummond's property interest may have to be acquired by the City in order to accomplish the project.

Councilman Drummond requests your written advice concerning (1) whether he may participate in the consideration and action regarding that part of the Transportation (Circulation) Element of the City's General Plan which relates specifically to the described recommended intersection project, and (2) whether he is disqualified from participation and voting with respect to the subsequent consideration and approval of the plans, specifications, contract or other matters implementing that particular intersection improvement project.

Councilman Drummond also has requested your written advice regarding whether the placement of his interest in this stock cooperative apartment in a trust would have any effect on his ability to participate and vote in connection with the above-described actions.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Very truly yours,

JOHN R. CALHOUN
CITY ATTORNEY

JRC:fl

cc: Councilman Douglas Drummond
